description: Cleans the current user's temporary folder.
safetyNote: Low risk. Temporary files are usually recreated as needed.

description: Cleans the Windows temp directory. Some files may be skipped if they are in use.
safetyNote: Windows may recreate some temporary files after updates or restarts.

description: Cleans current-user Windows Error Reporting queue, archive, and temp data.
safetyNote: Useful for troubleshooting only if you still need recent crash reports.
Explorer will rebuild thumbnail cache automatically when needed.

description: Cleans Windows icon cache databases for the current user.
safetyNote: Windows will rebuild icon cache automatically after restart or folder refresh.

description: Cleans common cache folders for Chrome, Edge, Brave, and Firefox profiles.
safetyNote: Browsers may recreate cache files quickly. Some files can stay locked while a browser is open.

description: Cleans DirectX and graphics shader cache folders for the current user.
safetyNote: Graphics drivers and games may rebuild shader cache after the next launch.

description: Removes application crash dump files from the current user profile.
safetyNote: Keep these if you are still debugging recent application crashes.

description: Cleans downloaded Windows Update packages that can accumulate over time.
safetyNote: Windows may need to redownload update files later. Use when reclaiming large amounts of space.

description: Cleans the Windows.old folder left behind after a major Windows upgrade.
safetyNote: Only remove this if you are sure you do not need to roll back a recent Windows upgrade.

description: Cleans cached Delivery Optimization files downloaded by Windows.
safetyNote: Windows may re-download these files later for updates or peer delivery.

description: Cleans Windows setup and upgrade log folders that can remain after installs and updates.
safetyNote: Keep these if you still need to troubleshoot a recent Windows installation or upgrade issue.

description: Scans and empties the current user's recycle bin on the system drive.
safetyNote: Irreversible. Keep it unchecked unless you have reviewed deleted items.
